problem.title: Swap Nodes in Pairs
problem.description: Given a linked list, swap every two adjacent nodes and return its head.
assumptions: If odd number of nodes, last node stays.

example.explanation: Swap every two adjacent nodes. 1↔2, 3↔4. Iterative or recursive.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Iterative [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: A dummy head node simplifies edge cases; a 'prev' pointer then repeatedly re-links each pair (first, second) so that second precedes first, and prev advances past both. Performing the swap with three pointer reassignments in the right order ensures no node is orphaned. The key insight is that the two swaps — connecting second's next to first's old next, then pointing second before first — must happen before prev advances.
solutions.code_python:
# class TreeNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.left=self.right=None
from typing import Optional

class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val: int = 0, next: 'Optional[ListNode]' = None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

def swap_pairs(head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
    dummy = ListNode(0)
    dummy.next = head
    prev = dummy

    while prev.next and prev.next.next:
        first = prev.next
        second = prev.next.next

        first.next = second.next
        second.next = first
        prev.next = second

        prev = first

    return dummy.next

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Recursive [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Base case: if the list has 0 or 1 nodes, return head unchanged. Otherwise, take the first two nodes, recursively solve the rest of the list, attach the result to the second node's next pointer, then point the second node back to the first. Return the second node as the new head. O(n) time O(n) stack space.
